task.title: Connecting a local project to a remote system
task.shortdesc: You can connect a local project to a remote system and map local files to remote data sets.

keywords.indexterm: connecting a local project to a remote system
local projectconnecting to a remote system

taskbody.context:
The remote synchronization function is deprecated.A local project is one in which resources are created and stored locally. You can create a local z/OS project, for example, when you want to use the z/OS tools with COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler source that is stored in a Git repository. Some tools, such as Enterprise Service Tools, create local projects that contain host-based resources. The following examples of local projects contain host-based resources:
A local z/OS project that contains COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler resources
A local clone of a Git repository that contains COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler resources
An Enterprise Service Tools single-service project that supports web services for CICS; SOAP for CICS; an IMS SOAP gateway; or batch, TSO, or z/OS UNIX System Services applications
While the resources in these types of projects are created locally, they are often intended for transfer to a remote system. Remote synchronization is a set of tools that you can use to quickly transfer resources from a local project to a remote system in a systematic, repeatable way. It includes the following tools:
A menu command for creating a connection from a local project to a remote system.
A mapping editor for associating local files with their remote counterparts.
Options for either manually or automatically synchronizing the project with the remote system.
